title: Ridsdalea grandis
intro: Ridsdalea grandis, also known as the "Giant Ridsdalea," is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ridsdalea grandis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ridsdalea grandis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Ridsdalea grandis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to Borneo and is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree. The plant is a member of the Melastomataceae family, which consists of over 150 genera of flowering plants. This family is native to tropical regions of the world, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. Ridsdalea grandis is a unique species within the Melastomataceae family.
